title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A romantic novel of manners by Jane Austen, following Elizabeth Bennet as she navigates issues of marriage, morality, and misconceptions — culminating in her relationship with the proud Mr. Darcy.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: The story of Victor Frankenstein, a young scientist who creates a sapient creature in an unorthodox experiment. One of the first works of science fiction and a timeless cautionary tale about the nature of creation.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: The fabulously wealthy Jay Gatsby and his obsessive love for Daisy Buchanan, set against the lavish backdrop of the Jazz Age. A quintessential American novel about the elusive dream of wealth and reinvention.

title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
author: Mark Twain
description: Huck Finn, a boy from Missouri, escapes his abusive father and embarks on a river journey down the Mississippi with Jim, a runaway enslaved man. A foundational work of American literature.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: Captain Ahab's obsessive quest to hunt the white sperm whale Moby Dick. A sweeping epic of adventure, fate, and humanity's struggle against nature, narrated by the sailor Ishmael.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
description: The epic journey home of the Greek hero Odysseus after the fall of Troy. Battling gods, monsters, and temptation over ten years, the Odyssey is one of the oldest and greatest works of Western literature.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: The story of orphan Jane Eyre, who overcomes hardship and heartbreak to find love and independence. A pioneering first-person narrative and cornerstone of English literature.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: Told through diary entries and letters, Dracula follows solicitor Jonathan Harker's encounter with the vampire Count Dracula and the group of heroes who attempt to stop his plans. The definitive vampire novel.

title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
author: Lewis Carroll
description: Young Alice falls through a rabbit hole into a bizarre fantasy world populated by peculiar creatures. A beloved classic of children's literature that has delighted readers of all ages for over 150 years.

title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
author: Mark Twain
description: The classic coming-of-age tale of young Tom Sawyer, his best friend Huckleberry Finn, and their adventures along the Mississippi River — from whitewashing fences to discovering treasure.

title: Sense and Sensibility
author: Jane Austen
description: The Dashwood sisters — sensible Elinor and passionate Marianne — navigate love, heartbreak, and society's expectations in Georgian England. Jane Austen's debut novel, as witty and wise as ever.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
description: Dorian Gray's portrait ages and bears the marks of his sins while he himself remains young and beautiful. Oscar Wilde's only novel is a dazzling exploration of vanity, corruption, and moral decay.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
description: Edmond Dantès is falsely imprisoned and escapes to become the wealthy and mysterious Count of Monte Cristo, plotting an elaborate revenge against those who wronged him. A thrilling epic of justice and betrayal.

title: A Tale of Two Cities
author: Charles Dickens
description: Set in London and Paris during the French Revolution, this sweeping novel of love, sacrifice, and resurrection follows Charles Darnay and Sydney Carton as their lives become entangled in turbulent times.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
description: Young Pip goes from being a poor orphan to a gentleman of 'great expectations' in Victorian England, discovering hard truths about wealth, class, and what it means to be a good person.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: Raskolnikov, a destitute student in St. Petersburg, commits a murder and then grapples with guilt, paranoia, and his own crumbling worldview. A masterwork of psychological depth and moral philosophy.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: An epic panorama of Russian society during the Napoleonic Wars, following the intertwined lives of five aristocratic families. Widely regarded as one of the greatest novels ever written.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: Anna Karenina, a beautiful Russian aristocrat, embarks on a doomed affair with the dashing Count Vronsky. Tolstoy's tragic masterpiece explores love, family, society, and the human need for meaning.

title: The Iliad
author: Homer
description: Homer's ancient epic chronicles the last weeks of the Trojan War, centering on the wrath of the Greek hero Achilles. One of the most influential works in all of Western literature.

title: Treasure Island
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: Young Jim Hawkins discovers a treasure map and sets sail to find the legendary bounty of the pirate Captain Flint — but the crew includes the cunning Long John Silver. The quintessential pirate adventure.

title: A Byte of Python
author: Swaroop C.H.
description: A free beginner's book on Python that teaches the language step-by-step from the ground up. Covers the basics, functions, modules, data structures, and object-oriented programming. Translated into over 25 languages.

title: Python Data Science Handbook
author: Jake VanderPlas
description: An essential reference for data scientists working with Python. Covers NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, and Scikit-Learn through practical examples and real-world datasets. The full text is freely available online.

title: Dive Into Python 3
author: Mark Pilgrim
description: A hands-on guide to Python 3 for experienced programmers. Covers native datatypes, comprehensions, generators, iterators, closures, and advanced topics like serialization and HTTP web services.

title: Introduction to Programming Using Java
author: David J. Eck
description: A comprehensive, freely available introductory programming textbook using Java. Covers objects, classes, algorithms, GUI programming with JavaFX, threads, networking, and data structures. Now in its 9th edition.

title: Think Java: How to Think Like a Computer Scientist
author: Allen B. Downey & Chris Mayfield
description: A concise introduction to Java programming for beginners. Emphasises computational thinking, problem solving, and software design. Covers variables, conditionals, loops, methods, objects, and arrays in a clear, readable style.

title: The Rust Programming Language
author: Steve Klabnik & Carol Nichols
description: The official Rust language book — affectionately known as 'The Book'. Covers ownership, borrowing, lifetimes, structs, enums, generics, traits, error handling, concurrency, and building command-line and web projects.

title: Pro Git
author: Scott Chacon & Ben Straub
description: The definitive guide to Git, written by GitHub co-founder Scott Chacon. Covers branching, merging, rebasing, distributed workflows, GitHub, submodules, and Git internals. Freely available on the official Git website.

title: Think Data Structures: Algorithms and Information Retrieval in Java
author: Allen B. Downey
description: Teaches data structures and algorithms using Java. Covers lists, maps, trees, graphs, sorting, and searching, with emphasis on practical applications in information retrieval and web search. Free online from Green Tea Press.

title: The Linux Command Line
author: William E. Shotts
description: A complete introduction to the Linux command line and shell scripting. Covers navigation, file manipulation, I/O redirection, pipelines, the environment, processes, text processing, shell scripting, and system administration. Free at linuxcommand.org.
